name: Iberogast
summary: Iberogast is best framed as a conservative research profile with mechanistic evidence and a focus on Digestive Health. Current workbook signals support mechanistic context around limited source-backed mechanism signals, not treatment claims.
description: Iberogast is best framed as a conservative research profile with mechanistic evidence and a focus on Digestive Health. Current workbook signals support mechanistic context around limited source-backed mechanism signals, not treatment claims.
mechanisms: Multi-herb preparation may modulate gastric motility
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

contraindications: Pre-existing liver disease (contains celandine which has been linked to rare cases of drug-induced liver injury)
signs of liver injury such as jaundice or dark urine or unusual fatigue (discontinue immediately and seek care)
chamomile or other Asteraceae-family plant allergy (possible cross-reactivity)
